results.title: LLM Stats: Compare API models by benchmarks, cost & capabilities | Product Hunt
results.content:
Lilou Lane

Really impressed by the vision here. Independent, reproducible benchmarks are critical for meaningful comparisons, and building a semi-private community adds a lot of trust. Looking forward to exploring the platform.

#### What needs improvement

Alex V
Ivan Zaparovanyi
Tim Whitacre
Rate a Meeting

Rate a Meeting

#### What's great

I believe they exist to data mine at this point. I signed up, verified my account, email, and personal identity - then they informed me they no longer support SaaS or Software Products. Confused, I reached out and asked why, and their rep told me in order to proceed, they needed access to my personal social media accounts.

#### What needs improvement

At this point, I'm pretty sure they are just scamming folks to mine their data. They also don't let you manually delete your account, you have to reach out to them directly - and then my account wasn't actually even deleted.

results.title: Aqua Voice vs Wispr Flow : Aqua Voice Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
Daniel Cahn
Ash 

Ash

@jakecrump Really good UX, really fast, and just does the best job by far at transcribing what I say correctly! Love that it's app-aware so it formats differently for email vs Slack, for example.

Chris Messina
Raycast

Raycast

Don't sleep on Monologue from Every, coming soon.

I also really like @TalkTastic. It's UI is unlike any of the others, and it's recently gotten a lot faster.

Luann Silva

@chrismessina have you tried out this new app called monologue? I couldn't find much legit info anywhere. if you’ve got the inside scoop or just some first impressions, would love to hear it!

Philip Grebowiec [...] Philip Grebowiec

@chrismessina  @luannsilva  I am using it and actually dictated this message with it. Looking good so far. I prefer the fact that you can have per-app or website profiles, which can change the tone and writing style that is ultimately found in the text that's processed. In addition, clicking on the menu bar shows you the last dictation so you can select both the raw input and processed output.

Brandon Gell
Monologue

Monologue

@chrismessina Appreciate it, Chris! Anyone can try out Monologue right now. It's in beta, but it's really good and is picking up steam on the design and feature side. Link here: 

Jake Crump
Product Hunt

Product Hunt [...] Aqua Voice
Jake Crump

# Aqua Voice vs Wispr Flow

26

I'm a big fan of voice dictation apps. In fact, I'm using one right now to write this very post (you'll have to wait till the end to see which one I'm using 😉)

The two main products I've used in this space are @Aqua Voice and @Wispr Flow. From talking to others, these are the two that I typically hear people mention using. In general, I hear a lot more people talk about using Wispr Flow.

I've personally found it super helpful using a dictation app for stuff like writing posts like this, quickly messaging in @Slack, writing up @Linear tasks, talking to @Claude by Anthropic, and even using it in my terminal with @Claude Code.
